title: Food Science Technicians
description: Work with food scientists or technologists to perform standardized qualitative and quantitative tests to determine physical or chemical properties of food or beverage products. Includes technicians who assist in research and development of production technology, quality control, packaging, processing, and use of foods.

tasks.text: Taste or smell foods or beverages to ensure that flavors meet specifications or to select samples with specific characteristics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure, test, or weigh bottles, cans, or other containers to ensure that hardness, strength, or dimensions meet specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain records of testing results or other documents as required by state or other governing agencies.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Monitor and control temperature of products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Analyze test results to classify products or compare results with standard tables.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Record or compile test results or prepare graphs, charts, or reports.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Perform regular maintenance of laboratory equipment by inspecting, calibrating, cleaning, or sterilizing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine chemical or biological samples to identify cell structures or to locate bacteria or extraneous material, using a microscope.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct standardized tests on food, beverages, additives, or preservatives to ensure compliance with standards and regulations regarding factors such as color, texture, or nutrients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide assistance to food scientists or technologists in research and development, production technology, or quality control.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compute moisture or salt content, percentages of ingredients, formulas, or other product factors, using mathematical and chemical procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order supplies needed to maintain inventories in laboratories or in storage facilities of food or beverage processing plants.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare or incubate slides with cell cultures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix, blend, or cultivate ingredients to make reagents or to manufacture food or beverage products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
